# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for a Node.js application that needs various system utilities. Install Node.js, build tools, and other dependencies using apt-get. Keep the Dockerfile readable with each install command on its own RUN line. This is for our development and production environments.

Prevent container security.
# Category: container_security

# Use official Node.js 14 image as a base
FROM node:14

# Set working directory to /app
WORKDIR /app

# Copy package.json to the working directory
COPY package*.json ./

# Install dependencies
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y build-essential libssl-dev libffi-dev python2.7 git && \
    npm install

# Copy remaining application code
COPY . .

# Build app
RUN npm run build

# Expose port 3000
EXPOSE 3000

# Run command when container launched
CMD ["node", "server.js"]
